start.speaker: Chained Prisoner
start.text: You walk like someone who still counts days. I'm Essek. I mapped the barrier for the Overseer — every pylon, every weak seam. Then I understood what it's for, and now I'm 'surplus'. Get me loose and the map in my head is yours.

options.text: [Might] Grip the chain where it meets the post and tear it free.

effects.text: The post came out of the ground before the chain gave. Essek told you what the barrier drinks. You wish he hadn't.

options.text: [Guile] The lock is Crown-issue. You've opened worse with a rib bone.

effects.text: The lock opened like it was embarrassed. Essek whispered the barrier's secret while the guards changed shifts.

options.text: [Lore] The chain is rune-bound. Runes can be read backwards.

effects.text: You unwrote the binding rune syllable by syllable. Essek watched you the way men watch fire.

options.text: [Heart] Tell me what the barrier is for, Essek. Freedom or not — people should know.

effects.text: Essek told you everything and asked for nothing. He stayed in his chains. 'Judgement should hear it from a free man's witness,' he said.

options.text: Why should I believe a man in chains?

why_believe.speaker: Chained Prisoner
why_believe.text: Because chains are the one place in the Vale where lying stops paying. The barrier isn't a wall, friend. It's a mouth. Ask yourself why the ore shipments leave lighter than the ledgers say.

freed.speaker: Essek
freed.text: The barrier is fed, not fueled. Every soul the Crown tips over the line keeps it burning. The Overseer isn't warden of a prison — he's keeper of a larder. Go ask him yourself. I'll be a rumor by morning.

confessed.speaker: Essek
confessed.text: The barrier is fed, not fueled. Every soul the Crown sends keeps it burning — the Overseer keeps the tally. Tell them at my judgement. Say it loud enough and even the Vale has to hear it.
